title: crinum stuhlmannii subsp. delagoense
intro: Crinum stuhlmannii subsp. delagoense is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, commonly known as the Delagoa lily. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of alkaloids such as crinum alkaloids and galanthamine, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Crinum stuhlmannii subsp. delagoense include its potential antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, fever, and other infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling the roots and leaves, and it is also used in the form of herbal teas and infusions.
